title: Kubernetes Pods in CrashLoopBackOff Across Payment Namespace
symptoms: All 12 pods in payment namespace showing CrashLoopBackOff status
Pod logs showing database authentication failure on startup
Kubernetes events showing repeated container restarts with increasing backoff
Payment API returning 503 Service Unavailable to all callers
Message queue depth growing rapidly for payment events
root_cause: Kubernetes secret rotation updated database credentials but pod deployments were pinned to the old secret version, causing authentication failures on every pod restart
resolution: Updated pod deployment manifests to reference the new secret version, performed rolling restart of all payment namespace pods, and drained the queued transactions
preventive_actions: Implement automatic secret version synchronization in deployments
Add pre-rotation validation checks for secret consumers
Create Kubernetes admission webhook to prevent secret version mismatches
Implement graceful degradation for database connectivity in payment services
Add payment namespace pod health monitoring with business-level alerts

timeline.event: All payment namespace pods enter CrashLoopBackOff state
P1 alert fires for payment service unavailability
Alex Johnson begins investigation, confirms all pods crashing
Pod logs reveal database authentication failures
Root cause identified as secret version mismatch from rotation
Deployment manifests updated to reference correct secret version
Rolling restart initiated, pods begin coming online
All pods healthy, queued transactions being processed

embedding_summary: Kubernetes pods in payment namespace entered CrashLoopBackOff after secret rotation updated database credentials without updating pod deployment references. Complete payment processing outage for 38 minutes affecting 5,600 transactions. Resolved by updating deployment manifests and performing rolling restart.
